{"id":490,"date":"2026-07-05T07:19:25","date_gmt":"2026-07-05T07:19:25","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?p=490"},"modified":"2026-07-10T10:41:19","modified_gmt":"2026-07-10T10:41:19","slug":"about-gpx-files","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/about-gpx-files\/","title":{"rendered":"About GPX Files"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

GPX files are field records created during documented hikes. They can be viewed in compatible mapping applications alongside the corresponding trail report.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Step 1<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Install a GPX-Compatible Map Application<\/h4>\n\n\n\n

The examples below use Organic Maps<\/strong>, a free offline mapping application that supports GPX files. Other GPX-compatible applications may also be used.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
